surface: aero-fighters — inhauma terrain grounding / helicopter altitude
title: aero-inhauma-invisible-mountains
aero-inhauma-support-missiles-never-fire

expected:
Tocar a pista em voo deve resultar em pouso (sink baixo) ou crash
(sink alto) — nunca um limbo sem transição.
notes:
O floor-clamp do caminho airborne (`src/player.js:518-529`) força
`y = contact.height + 0.9` sobre superfície de aeroporto, enquanto
`checkTerrainCollision` (`src/world.js:190`) devolve `null` quando `contact.safe`.
Touchdown só é avaliado em `RETURN_TO_BASE` (`src/player.js:553`). Solução: máquina
de contato única (WS-1 do audit `specs/audits/2026-06-12T220815Z/`).

**Fix (2026-06-12, release aero-fighters-uplift-v1):** Wave 1 (commit eb13fba): máquina de contato em player.js — touchdown oportunista / hard-landing bounce / crash roteado; estado congelado impossível (U-AC-3).
repro:
desert → decolar → nariz para baixo até tocar a pista voando. O avião
fica "colado" deslizando no chão para sempre.
symptom:
Em estado `AIRBORNE`, descer até a pista gruda o avião em y=0.9 com
z congelado (observado em ≈−260, borda sul da pista do desert), speed 80, sem crash,
sem pouso, sem recuperação possível. Verificado ao vivo 2× (probes Playwright).
title: aero-airborne-floor-glue

expected: Decolagem possível em todos os mapas jogáveis.
notes:
Liftoff gate exige `surface === 'runway'` (`src/player.js:355-358`), mas
`classifyGroundContact`/`getAirportForMap` só conhecem aeroportos do desert e do
inhauma (`src/landing-zones.js:23-29`, `src/airport.js:31-33`). Rio é incoerente:
`rioHeightAt` consulta o aeroporto do desert. Agravante: `updateGroundRoll` não tem
velocidade terminal (accel 18 > friction 4) — groundSpeed cresce sem limite.
Solução proposta: WS-2 do audit `specs/audits/2026-06-12T220815Z/`.

**Fix (2026-06-12, release aero-fighters-uplift-v1):** Wave 2 (commit eb13fba): pista costeira islands (ADR-U2) + registro de aeroportos por mapa + pista rio; liftoff verificado nos 4 mapas (U-AC-1).
repro:
`index.html?map=islands` → Space → segurar W (e ArrowDown). Observar
`window.game.missionRealism.sortie.state` — nunca sai de TAXI_OUT.
surface: aero-fighters — sortie FSM / landing-zones / maps islands+rio
symptom:
No mapa `islands` (e `rio`) com mission realism, o avião nunca decola.
Verificado ao vivo: 8 s de throttle máximo + rotação → estado `TAXI_OUT`, y=0.9,
speed **106,5 m/s**, contact `water`. O jogo fica soft-locked esquiando na água.
title: aero-islands-realism-softlock

expected:
O jogador descobre a decolagem em segundos (quality-bar: "controles
descobertos em segundos"). Input natural deve funcionar OU haver callout claro.
notes:
Liftoff gate escuta exclusivamente `input.pitchDown` (`src/player.js:355-358`).
A dica existe só no overlay de texto inicial. Agravante: groundSpeed sem limite
(106+ m/s) mascara o problema — parece que "falta velocidade". Fix → WS-4 do audit
`specs/audits/2026-06-12T220815Z/`: aceitar ↑ ou ↓ para rotação no solo + callout
"ROTATE" no HUD ao atingir V_ROTATE; grill: manter esquema invertido ou torná-lo
opcional.

**Fix (2026-06-12, release aero-fighters-uplift-v1):** Wave 3 (ADR-U1): no solo ↑ OU ↓ rotacionam (player.js liftoff gate); AC-5 atualizado e verde.
repro: desert → Space → segurar W + ArrowUp. Nada acontece, sem nenhum feedback.
surface: aero-fighters — takeoff rotation input (player.js liftoff gate)
symptom:
"O avião não voa." Segurar ↑ (input natural de subir) com throttle
máximo NUNCA decola — verificado live: 8 s de W + ArrowUp → preso em y=0.9 a
106 m/s. Só a tecla ↓ (esquema invertido) rotaciona e decola (3 s → AIRBORNE).
title: aero-rotate-key-undiscoverable

expected:
Mar só existe onde o mapa tem água (islands, orla do rio). Impacto na
água → splash + afundamento; impacto na terra → explosão/skid.
notes:
`src/world.js:191` — `if (jetPosition.y < 3) return 'SEA'` incondicional.
`classifyGroundContact` até distingue água (`src/landing-zones.js:31`, só islands),
mas o caminho de crash não a consulta. Pedido explícito do operador (2026-06-12):
colisão com mar ≠ colisão com terra; avião deve cair com fumaça e explodir no solo,
ou afundar no mar. Solução: WS-1 + WS-5 do audit
`specs/audits/2026-06-12T220815Z/`.

**Fix (2026-06-12, release aero-fighters-uplift-v1):** Waves 1+5: surfaceInfoAt roteia crash por kind (GROUND/WATER/MOUNTAIN); água afunda com splash, terra explode com cicatriz (U-AC-2, U-AC-4).
repro:
desert → decolar → mergulhar no piso fora da pista. Overlay exibe
"IMPACTO NO MAR".
symptom:
Colidir com o chão plano de QUALQUER mapa abaixo de y=3 produz crash
classificado como `'SEA'` → overlay "IMPACTO NO MAR" no meio do deserto/cidade.
Não há diferenciação física mar vs terra (afundar vs explodir).
title: aero-sea-label-on-land

expected:
O mapa selecionado pelo botão é o mapa ativo para TODAS as camadas
(visual, colisão, aeroporto, layout de alvos).
notes:
`src/missions.js:55` e `:92` — `if (!game.runtime?.map) game.activeMap =
'desert'` sobrescreve a seleção feita por `window.selectMap()` (main.js). Os probes
anteriores usaram `?map=` e por isso viram o soft-lock (bug
`aero-islands-realism-softlock`) em vez deste split-brain. Fix → WS-2 do audit
`specs/audits/2026-06-12T220815Z/` (registro por mapa; remover o override).

**Fix (2026-06-12, release aero-fighters-uplift-v1):** Wave 2 (commit eb13fba): override removido de startGame/restartGame; activeMap segue o botão selecionado (U-AC-1 asserts map).
repro:
abrir index.html (sem query param) → clicar MAR DO SUL → Space →
`window.game.activeMap` === 'desert'.
surface: aero-fighters — missions.js startGame / map selection UI
symptom:
Selecionar QUALQUER mapa pelos botões do menu (MAR DO SUL / RIO /
INHAUMA) roda o jogo com física/colisão do DESERT: `startGame()` força
`game.activeMap = 'desert'` quando não há `?map=` na URL. Verificado live: islands
via botão → mundo oceano renderizado, mas contact='runway' invisível do desert no
meio do mar; decolagem de pista fantasma.
title: aero-startgame-forces-desert-activemap

expected:
Ao voltar à base depois de um abate (com vidas restantes), o avião
recoloca no aeroporto e RETOMA a decolagem — pode voar de novo.
repro:
Jogar `?map=inhauma`, tomar dano até o mayday, deixar cair/explodir (ou
ejetar com J). O avião reaparece no aeroporto mas não taxia nem decola.

**Root cause:** `_ejectAndRespawn()` (queda no solo) chamava `respawnJet()` para
recolocar o avião na zona de serviço, mas NÃO resetava a máquina de surtida — ela
permanecia em `MAYDAY`. O caminho de ejeção manual (main.js) deixava o avião em
`NEXT_SORTIE_READY` no ar. Em nenhum dos dois casos o `auto-taxi` era rearmado, então
a sequência automática táxi→decolagem nunca rodava. `MAYDAY` não é um `GROUND_STATE`,
então `updatePlayer` tratava o avião (parado no solo do aeroporto) como se estivesse
em voo — sem nunca reentrar no fluxo de decolagem.
surface: aero-fighters / sortie state machine (player.js, main.js, sortie-state.js)
symptom:
Ao ser destruído (HP a zero → mayday → queda) e voltar ao aeroporto, o
avião ficava PARADO e não decolava mais. A surtida ficava travada.
title: aero-respawn-cannot-takeoff

repro:
`?map=inhauma`, seguir qualquer estrada não-anelar até a ponta: MG-238
(início SO), AMG-0360 (norte), MG-060 (as duas pontas) simplesmente paravam.

**Expected (operador):** Nenhuma estrada pode terminar no nada. Estradas são contínuas
e seguem o mapa; no pior caso, ENTRAM NUM TÚNEL numa encosta. O terreno de inhauma é
"infinito" (chunks reciclados seguem o jogador) → não há borda de mapa, então toda
ponta aberta precisa virar túnel numa encosta.
symptom:
Estradas terminavam como um TOCO de fita em terreno plano — "acabavam no
nada". A primeira passada de túneis (WS-2) só cobriu 2 pontas; as demais paravam secas.
A ponta sul da MG-060 terminava DENTRO da água (represa), em altura 0.
title: aero-roads-end-in-nothing

expected: Todo corpo puxa dentro da sua influência; perto do par binário os DOIS parceiros puxam (campo binário real) e não existe zona morta de gravidade nula.
notes: Corrigido na mesma sessão (2026-07-01): fallback argmax-aceleração fora de todos os SOIs + soma dos dois parceiros binários quando o dominante é um deles (gravity.js). Verificado live: dominant=Buraco Negro, gravMag~2.8e7 u/s² perto do horizonte.
repro: Voar do sistema solar em direção ao binário (goTo blackhole no debug): entre ~560k e ~590k do Sol, gravMag=0; perto do par, dominant é sempre a estrela de nêutrons.
symptom: O buraco negro nunca puxa a nave: (1) fora de todos os SOIs a gravidade era NULA (zona morta entre o SOI do Sol e o do binário); (2) dentro do binário a regra 'menor SOI contendo a nave vence' escolhia SEMPRE a estrela de nêutrons (SOI 200k < BH 230k, sobrepostos), então o BH jamais era dominante.
title: Space-war: buraco negro sem atração nenhuma (patched-conics quebrado no binário)

expected: Solar glare should be local and physically plausible: visible near/through the Sun, distance/angle attenuated, occlusion-aware where possible, and not a universe-wide overlay.
notes: Evidence provided by operator in workspace bug-space-war screenshots. Likely related to the Three.js Lensflare attached to the Sun point light rather than the nuke full-screen flash.
repro: Open space-war, start flight, navigate far from the Solar System toward another system or compact-object target, then look in the direction of the Sun; screenshots show the flare still covering most of the screen at multi-million-unit distances.
symptom: A huge warm lens-flare/glare overlay appears while looking toward the Sun from far outside the Solar System, including while navigating toward other systems, washing out the ship and skybox.
title: Space War solar flare appears across the universe

expected: Exactly one Pages deploy mechanism: either switch repo Pages settings to build_type=workflow (making pages.yml authoritative) or delete the redundant pages.yml workflow (legacy branch deploy already publishes the static site).
notes: Operator decision between the two fixes. Diagnosed 2026-07-03 during space-war closure CI watch; failure also explains the earlier red deploy after the PR #10/#11 back-to-back merges. Redacted.
repro: Push any commit to main; observe the custom workflow's Deploy step fail while 'pages build and deployment' succeeds. Pages API shows build_type: legacy, source branch main.
surface: GitHub Actions workflow pages.yml (Deploy to GitHub Pages)
symptom: Every push to main runs BOTH the automatic 'pages build and deployment' (legacy mode, succeeds, actually publishes) AND the custom 'Deploy to GitHub Pages' workflow, whose actions/deploy-pages step fails with 'Deployment failed, try again later'. Red run on every main push; site content is unaffected (legacy deploy publishes).
title: Custom Pages deploy workflow always fails: conflicts with legacy branch-based Pages mode

expected: System keys consistent after migration: nav and map filter b.system==='solar'.
notes: Introduced by space-war-celestial-components-v1 (system.js registers with SYSTEMS key). Fix scheduled in space-war-ballistic-war-v1.
repro: Start flight in solar system, press T repeatedly: planets/local bodies of the solar system are missing from the cycle list.
symptom: Celestial migration registers solar bodies with system='solar', but nav.js localTargets maps solar→'home' and map.js labels check b.system==='home' — local solar targets vanish from T-cycling and solar map labels are suppressed. Masked because primaries+mission targets still work.
title: nav/map still filter system 'home' after celestial migration renamed it 'solar'

expected: A pulsar is one of the most luminous compact objects known (Crab: spin-down luminosity ~1.3e5 L_sun); up close it should be a blinding point source with visible torus/jets, not a dim sphere
notes: Fix belongs to the physics-fidelity review round; see review artifacts
repro: space-war -> map M -> travel to binary system -> approach the pulsar; body reads dim/ill-defined at close range
symptom: Operator playtest: neutron star is not shining; very close to it the body can barely be seen. Core is a small MeshBasicMaterial sphere + short-falloff glint sprite; relies on bloom threshold to saturate.
title: Neutron star barely visible up close — pulsar should outshine everything

expected: Stars are unresolved point sources: fixed ~1.5-3px PSF core whose BRIGHTNESS follows flux (F ~ L/d^2 mapped through the magnitude chain), fading in at the wrap-cell boundary, growing in size only when the star's angular size exceeds ~1px on a close flyby (planetarium-standard rendering: Stellarium StelSkyDrawer, Celestia, Gaia Sky instanced billboard quads).
notes: Root cause at src/starfield.js:83. Fix recipe + references in report 2026-07-04T043851Z-space-war-simulation-tech-review.html (appendix A). Recommend instanced quads over GL_POINTS: WebGL spec only guarantees 1px max point size and WebGPU has no point size at all.
repro: Start game, target a body in another system, engage journey (T then Z), watch the starfield: stars enter view already at maximum point size as equal-size discs.
symptom: During interstellar travel, stars appear as uniform small circles of a fixed size from the moment they become visible. gl_PointSize = clamp(uBase*3000/z, 0.7, 7.0) saturates ~85-90% of visible stars at the 7px max (uBase=900 saturates below z~385k while the wrap half-cell is 450k), and the brightness term vBoost has no 1/d^2 flux attenuation and no wrap-boundary fade-in.
title: Interstellar starfield stars pop in as fixed-size 7px circles instead of dim points that brighten on approach

expected: Angular-size honesty: a body of radius R at distance d subtends theta ~ 2R/d; below ~1px it must render as a photometric PSF point (brightness from L/d^2, mesh/corona culled), cross-fading to the disc/mesh at ~2px with hysteresis; glare/flare size and intensity must scale with received flux, never with a fixed world size or a screen-space floor (Celestia/Gaia Sky/Stellarium ladder; Spencer 1995 glare model).
notes: Root cause in src/celestial/stars.js buildVisual (coronaScale/halo constants, FLARE_FULL/FLARE_CUTOFF floor 0.22). Fix recipe + references in report 2026-07-04T043851Z-space-war-simulation-tech-review.html (appendix A section 3). Side benefit: culling distant star meshes removes additive overdraw and FBM fragments.
repro: From interstellar space or a neighboring system, look toward any system primary: its glow subtends several degrees regardless of distance instead of collapsing to a bright point.
symptom: Even from very far away, the main stars of each system look big. Corona/halo sprites are world-scaled (Sun corona = radius x 4.2 ~ 462k units diameter, still ~6.6 degrees of screen at 4M units; neutron-star halo = radius x 90) and the Lensflare 640px screen-space element floors at 0.22x (~141px) until the 4.2M cutoff. Sub-pixel suns still draw their full mesh + FBM shader.
title: Main system stars render oversized at any distance - world-scaled corona/halo sprites and lens-flare floor, no photometric point/disc LOD

expected: A young pulsar is a brilliant point source (Crab spin-down ~1.2e5 L_sun): it should render as the game's brightest photometric point (fixed-size PSF core, brightness from L/d^2, glare ~ sqrt(I/Isat)), blue-white and strobing at the 30 Hz optical signature, visible from any system - switching to the resolved disc anatomy only when the true angular size exceeds ~2px.
notes: Fix ships in release space-war-photometric-stars-v1 (point/disc LOD + scene-level far-star layer, lum override 80 documented as compressed like masses). Review report 2026-07-04T043851Z-space-war-simulation-tech-review.html appendix A.
repro: Start flight at Earth; look toward the binary system (or fly to it): no distinct pulsar visible until practically on top of it. Distinct from resolved bug space-war-neutron-star-too-dim (light emission, fixed in physics-fidelity-v1) - this is apparent visibility at range.
symptom: Operator: 'Review the neutron star. I can barely see it.' The NS disc (radius 30) subtends less than 1 pixel beyond ~36k units, so across the binary system (separation 140k) and from other systems it is effectively invisible; its glow sprites are world-scaled (corona x26 = 780u, halo x90 = 2700u) and its system group is culled beyond 1.15x system radius, leaving only a generic fixed-size beacon sprite.
title: Neutron star is barely visible at system/interstellar range - sub-pixel disc with no photometric point representation

expected: Bodies grow as you APPROACH (perspective), small at distance - honest theta=2R/d. Operator: bodies should be big near collision but small as we travel, like nature.
notes: Fix: revert static radius inflation, keep close-approach experience via proximity; re-derive mu/SOI/moon-orbits/periods (Kepler sqrt(k^3/f) law); rewrite wall-scale test ACs (operator explicitly reversed the earlier x10-static demand).
repro: Fly anywhere in the solar system and look at the Sun or any planet.
surface: space-war config.js scale pipeline (approach-scale + wall-scale rounds)
symptom: Operator screenshots 10-34/10-37: Sun fills a third of the screen from 286k (subtends ~30 deg; real sun 0.53 deg), Earth subtends ~16 deg from 157k, Saturn a huge ringed disc from Earth. Static radius inflation (x22/x9/x6 approach round, then x10 wall round, Sun x2x5) broke all apparent-volume proportions.
title: Apparent sizes of suns/planets/moons are wildly inflated - Sun ~29 deg from inside Venus orbit, Earth 16 deg from 157k

expected: Different systems are effectively light-years away: from another system, at most a faint photometric point/glow - never a disc. IT WOULD BE IMPOSSIBLE TO SEE SATURN FROM THE BLACK HOLE.
notes: Fix: push system centers ~x5 apart, cull solar like other systems, drop Betelgeuse alwaysVisible, re-enable solar photometric glow, retune photometric gauges, extend far plane for point sprites only.
repro: Journey [Z] to the binary; look back: Sun and Saturn are resolvable discs.
surface: space-war config SYSTEMS ring + celestial/system.js cull (solar never culled)
symptom: Operator screenshots 02-39/10-46/10-47: Saturn ring disc, the Sun and planet parade visible from interstellar space and from the black-hole system. System ring sits at 4.5-5.8M units while solar SOI itself spans 4.7M - systems practically touch; updateSOIView never culls the solar system; Betelgeuse is alwaysVisible.
title: Bodies of other star systems visible as discs across interstellar space - systems are adjacent, not light-years apart

expected: Operator: BH 3x bigger, accretion disk 5x bigger, per reference art - spiral streaked disk, bright inner annulus, jets; remnant fades in from far instead of popping.
notes: Fix: rs 160->480, disk outer 3200->16000 (ISCO 3rs kept), photon ring 2.6rs, tideKillR ~16rs, spiral-streak disk shader, jets on stellar BH, spiral accretion stream (curved tube), remnant distance-fade.
repro: Fly to the binary system and view the black hole.
symptom: Screenshots 10-45/10-46: BH is a minuscule orange donut at 145k; accretion stream is a straight rigid cylinder slicing the sky; supernova remnant (the plasma ball) hard-culled at 1.15x system radius so it appears late and vanishes on entry. References (3 Sagittarius images): disk dominates 10-20x the shadow, spiral turbulent streaks, white-hot inner rim, bipolar jet.
title: Black hole visual rejected - tiny donut vs reference art; accretion stream renders as rigid pipe; remnant plasma ball pops

expected: Bigger core (visual radius ~3x), blinding white core saturating bloom, long bright needle jets at the poles, visible dipole cage, fast spin, strong halo.
notes: Fix in space-war-true-proportions-v1 alongside the BH redesign; lum/photometric far-point already shipped in photometric-stars-v1.
repro: Fly to the binary system and view the neutron star.
symptom: Operator: 'create a decent neutron star... must bright and spin and must have a plasma jet in the poles'. Current pulsar reads as a small dot with subtle jets at system scale (screenshots 10-45/10-46). References (2 images): blinding white-blue sphere, huge soft blue halo, thin needle-like polar jets piercing both poles, dipole field-line cage.
title: Neutron star visual rejected - must be bigger, blinding, spinning, with polar plasma jets per reference art

expected: G launches the luminous gravity tracer, H launches the Higgs bomb (both gated by flight/landed as designed); unknown action names in onAction must fail loudly or auto-register, never silently drop handlers.
notes: Fix in space-war-true-proportions-v1 rc-2: onAction auto-creates lists ((listeners[name] ??= []).push) killing the silent-drop class; new e2e uses REAL KeyG/KeyH keypresses (lesson: debug-call coverage is not input coverage).
repro: Take off, press G or H: nothing happens, no toast. Press F: nuke fires (nuke key exists in the map). Shipped in space-war-physics-fidelity-v1: tests and live verification exercised __swDebug.launchGravBomb()/launchHiggs() directly - only the nuke was verified via a real keypress.
symptom: Operator: of the 3 weapons only nukes work; the Higgs bomb does nothing and projectiles are never attracted by gravity wells (wells never spawn since [H] is dead). Root cause: input.js listeners map is a fixed literal without gravbomb/higgs keys; onAction uses listeners[name]?.push(fn) which silently no-ops for unknown names, so main.js handlers for gravbomb/higgs are never registered and emit() finds no listeners.
title: [G] tracer and [H] Higgs bomb keyboard actions never fire - listeners map lacks their entries, onAction silently no-ops

expected: Operator spec: 30% accel / 40% at max speed / 30% decel; stars crossed near our trajectory GROW before passing (closer approach = bigger); strong forward concentration (aberration) with brightness (beaming) at near-c; stars elongate/streak as we pass; parallax dramatic; collisions with stars impossible during the journey.
notes: Fix in space-war-interstellar-experience-v1: trapezoid profile, beta_visual to ~0.995 at cruise, per-star pseudo-radius growth on close passage, tangential streak elongation proportional to apparent angular rate, stronger beaming, journey collision immunity (reverses the rc-1 collision-abort QA fix per explicit operator demand). Research brief with references first.
repro: Engage [Z] to another system and watch the corridor at peak speed.
symptom: Operator: at near-light speed nothing conveys it. (1) Stars near screen center that we are about to pass close-by stay tiny instead of GROWING on approach (all stars read as equal small points); (2) the headlight concentration of stars toward the flight direction is barely noticeable and star brightness/elongation as we pass is absent; (3) parallax reads weak; (4) journey profile has no max-speed cruise (pure 50/50 brachistochrone); (5) collision with bodies can abort the journey.
title: Interstellar journey feels flat: no close-passage star growth, aberration/beaming barely evident, no streaking, collision aborts, no cruise phase
